{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about ganglionic transmission. From the provided documents, we learn that nicotinic ganglion receptors found in the autonomic ganglia are involved in both sympathetic and parasympathetic transmission. These receptors are neuronal nicotinic (Nn) cholinergic receptors, and they are on postganglionic efferent neurons. The activation of the Nn receptor is mediated by the release of acetylcholine from preganglionic efferent neurons. Therefore, ganglionic transmission is mediated by postsynaptic nicotinic receptors.", "answer_choice": "D"}
